verifiedBehavior.fact: The native Start menu horizontal size resolves to capacities for one, two, or three tile-group columns rather than persisting an arbitrary width.
verifiedBehavior.evidence: User comparison against the native Win10 Start menu on 2026-07-20.

verifiedBehavior.fact: The native Start menu vertical size follows the pointer continuously within a bounded range.
verifiedBehavior.evidence: User comparison against the native Win10 Start menu on 2026-07-20.

verifiedBehavior.fact: TileStart exposes top, right, and top-right resize hit targets across the full window surface; horizontal release snaps to one of three group-width targets while vertical size remains continuous.
verifiedBehavior.evidence: Win10 150% DPI manual validation accepted by the user on 2026-07-20.

verifiedBehavior.fact: TileStart lets the native resize loop track the pointer, then animates the released width to the nearest legal target from CompositionTarget.Rendering and applies the existing group-reorder motion after layout changes.
verifiedBehavior.evidence: Manual validation accepted as sufficiently close on 2026-07-20.

unresolved: The native Start menu's exact horizontal transition thresholds have not been isolated from StartUI binaries.
The native window-edge easing and duration have not been isolated. TileStart's 180 ms cubic ease-out is an independently implemented runtime-calibrated approximation.
Resize behavior still requires separate validation for non-bottom taskbars, multiple monitors, and DPI values other than 150%.
